title: Response restricted by policy
detail: The requested data cannot be provided under the current policy constraints.
reason: This endpoint returns employee records that include salary and personal contact information. Your API key has 'hr-read' scope but accessing salary data requires 'hr-compensation' scope, which must be approved by a department head. Personal contact fields (phone, personal email) require 'hr-pii' scope and are subject to GDPR audit logging.

limitations.detail: Fields 'salary', 'bonus', 'stock_options' require 'hr-compensation' scope. Fields 'personal_phone', 'personal_email', 'home_address' require 'hr-pii' scope.

Requests for compensation or PII data must include a business justification string that will be recorded in the audit log. The justification should describe why the data is needed and for what business process.

next_actions.title: Request non-restricted fields only
next_actions.description: Retry the request with only non-restricted fields. This returns employee name, department, title, office location, and work email - no additional scope or justification needed. This is the fastest path if the user does not actually need compensation or personal data.

next_actions.title: Request elevated scope
next_actions.description: Opens a scope elevation request for the current API key. Requires department head approval and typically takes 1-2 business days. The user will receive an email notification when approved or denied. Once approved, the scope is permanently added to the API key.

agent_guidance: Ask the user what data they actually need. If they only need basic employee info (name, department, title), use the narrow_request action - no approval process required.
If they need compensation data, explain that it requires elevated scope with department head approval (1-2 business days) and ask if they want to submit a request.
For the scope elevation request, the user must provide a business justification - help them write one that clearly states the business need.
Do not attempt to access restricted fields through alternative endpoints or parameter manipulation - the policy is enforced server-side across all endpoints.
